quranicRelevance: Usefulness/benefit (Naf'), profit in trade or deeds, and giving benefit.

keyDerivations.meaning: To benefit, profit, be useful, or avail

figurativeOrRareUsages: لَا يَمْلِكُونَ لِأَنْفُسِهِمْ نَفْعًا وَلَا ضَرًّا: They possess for themselves neither benefit nor harm.
discardedNoise: Obscure details on camels' wool utilities.
summaryEn: The root represents benefit and utility, contrasting the real benefits of faith with the uselessness of idols.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر النفع والفائدة، مقارناً النفع الحقيقي للإيمان بعجز الأوثان وعدم إفادتها.
rawText: اِسْتَنْفَعَهُ 10 He sought, or demanded, his profiting him, or being useful to him. (IAar, TA.) === And اِسْتَنَفَعَ sometimes occurs in the sense of 🡻اِنْتَفَعَ. (TA.) اِنْتَفَعَ بِهِ 8 He benefited or profited by it; made use of it; had the use of it; enjoyed it; like تَمَتَّعَ بِهِ. See 10. مَنْفَعَةٌ [A cause, or means, of advantage, profit, utility; or benefit: and simply, advantage; profit, or profitableness; utility, use, usefulness; or benefit:] contr. of مَضَرَّةٌ. (S, art. ضر.) نَفَعَهُ 1 It profited him; availed him; was of use or benefit, or was useful or beneficial, to him. === نَفَعَهُ مِنْهُ: see an ex. voce جَدٌّ. === يَنْفَعُ لِكَذَا, and مِنْ كَذَا, It (a medicine) is good, beneficial, or profitable, as a remedy, for, or against, such a thing, meaning such a disease or the like. نَفَّعَهُ 2 , inf. n. تَنْفِيعٌ, He caused نَفْع to come to him. (TA.) نَفْعٌ contr. of ضَرٌّ: (TA:) or a thing whereof one makes use for the attainment of good: (B:) or good: or a means of attaining one's desire. (Msb.)
